claim: Dense Passage Retrieval uses learned dense representations to retrieve passages for open-domain question answering.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: Dense Passage Retrieval relies exclusively on BM25 and does not use neural encoders.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: ColBERT uses late interaction between contextualized query and document token representations.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: REALM stores all factual knowledge only in model parameters and does not retrieve external documents.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: RAG combines parametric memory with a non-parametric document index.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: Self-RAG always retrieves documents for every generation step and never critiques its own outputs.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: CRAG evaluates retrieved documents and can trigger corrective retrieval actions.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: RAGAS is designed for reference-free evaluation of retrieval-augmented generation pipelines.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: ALCE focuses on helping language models generate text with verifiable citations.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: FActScore evaluates factual precision by decomposing long-form text into atomic facts.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: SciFact contains scientific claims paired with supporting or refuting evidence and rationales.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: SciFact uses only one label and does not distinguish support from refutation.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: FEVER includes supported, refuted, and not-enough-information style claim labels.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: SPECTER learns scientific document representations without using citation information.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: Longformer reduces the quadratic cost of self-attention for long documents.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: Lost in the Middle finds that language models perform best when relevant evidence is always placed in the middle of the context.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: The evaluated papers report that retrieval-augmented generation improved crop yields by 42 percent in 2023.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: The corpus demonstrates that all retrieval systems use identical model architectures and training objectives.
label: CONTRADICTED
